output.final_deliverable:
I visited the Wikipedia Main Page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page) and extracted the available link sample from the merged results. The merged results include a links_count of 20 and a links_sample containing 3 URLs. Below are the links the merged results provided with the information available from those results. Note: the merged results did not include anchor text beyond the URL samples nor did they include 1–2 sentence descriptions for these pages, so descriptions are marked as missing.

1) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents
   Link text/title (from URL): Wikipedia:Contents
   Description: Not provided in the merged results.

2) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Current_events
   Link text/title (from URL): Portal:Current_events
   Description: Not provided in the merged results.

3) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:Random
   Link text/title (from URL): Special:Random
   Description: Not provided in the merged results.

Missing items and next steps:
- The request asked for 10 links; the merged results only supplied a sample of 3 link URLs. Seven additional links are missing from the provided results.
- For each link the user requested a brief (1–2 sentence) description; those descriptions are not present in the merged results and therefore cannot be supplied from the available evidence.

If you want, I can (A) re-visit the Wikipedia Main Page to collect a full set of 10 links with anchor text and fetch each page to produce the requested 1–2 sentence descriptions, or (B) use the three sample links above and fetch descriptions for them. Please tell me which you prefer.
output.action_summary: Visited https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page (merged results). The merged results reported 20 links and provided a sample of 3 URLs: Wikipedia:Contents, Portal:Current_events, and Special:Random. The merged results did not include anchor text beyond the URL samples nor any page descriptions, and seven requested links are missing. I listed the three available links and noted that descriptions and the remaining 7 links are not present in the merged results. Suggested next steps: re-visit the page to collect the full 10 links and fetch descriptions, or fetch descriptions for the three sample links.

result.title: Select 10 links from Main Page results and plan per-link visits

details.intent: From the list of candidate links produced by the Main Page visit, choose 10 distinct Wikipedia article pages to summarize, prioritizing variety and prominence.
selection_criteria: Prefer internal article pages (not portals, talk, or special pages).
Include a mix of prominence (featured article or in-the-news) and topical variety (history, science, culture, current events).
Avoid duplicate topics and disambiguation pages; prefer pages with substantive article content.
target_facts: selected_links: array of exactly 10 items {url_exact_from_visit_result, link_text, section, reason_for_selection}
verification_steps: For each chosen link, ensure the URL originated from the Main Page visit output (use exact URL from that output).
Ensure each chosen URL is unique and resolves to an article (not a Special: or Talk: or Help: namespace).
details.rationale: We cannot construct or guess page URLs. This think node uses the Main Page visit output to pick 10 exact URLs to visit. It organizes and documents why each was chosen so subsequent visit nodes will know which pages to fetch for descriptions.

48763c8e-f7d7-4b47-a7f3-ee2ef7f16f57.title: Visit Wikipedia Main Page and extract candidate links

details.intent: Collect in-page links (URLs and anchor text) from the Wikipedia Main Page so we can choose 10 article pages to summarize.
target_facts: List of internal article links found on the page (each: url, link_text, surrounding context/snippet).
Sections where each link appears (e.g., Featured article, In the news, On this day, Today's featured picture, Did you know…).
At least 20 candidate internal links so we can pick the best 10 for summary.
details.extraction_plan: Download full page content and parse the main content areas. Extract all internal links (hrefs starting with /wiki/ or full en.wikipedia.org links) found in major Main Page modules (featured article, in the news, on this day, did you know, today's featured picture). For each link capture: exact URL, anchor/link text, and a short surrounding text snippet (up to ~200 characters) to help select links. Output links in order of page prominence (module order).
measurable_output_fields: candidates: [{url: string, link_text: string, section: string, context_snippet: string}]
verification_steps: Confirm the visit returned HTTP 200 and HTML content.
Confirm extracted URLs are valid HTTP(S) URLs and point to en.wikipedia.org or are relative /wiki/ paths (normalize to full URLs).
Confirm at least 20 distinct candidate links were collected; if fewer, widen extraction to more page areas.
details.rationale: The Main Page must be visited to find the exact link URLs (the mandate requires visiting specific pages). This node produces the exact URLs (in _links_inline or parsed anchors) that subsequent nodes will consume to visit each target page for 1-2 sentence descriptions. Doing this first prevents guessing or constructing URLs.
details.parent_goal: Go to the Wikipedia main page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page) and collect 10 links from the page. For each link, provide: the link URL, the link text/title, and a brief description (1-2 sentences) of what the page is about. Organize the results clearly.

9b3e8086-277a-467b-ac02-ccb2ac0023c7.title: Save selection to memory for downstream visits

details.intent: Persist the chosen 10 links (exact URLs and metadata) so the execution engine can spawn visit nodes to fetch each page and produce 1-2 sentence descriptions.
verification_steps: Confirm that the saved value contains exactly 10 entries and that each entry includes url and link_text.
Confirm memory write succeeded (acknowledgement from save tool).
details.rationale: Storing the selection guarantees reproducible downstream processing: separate visit nodes (one per selected URL) will read this memory to perform page visits and extract 1-2 sentence descriptions. This keeps data flow explicit and prevents re-parsing the Main Page multiple times.
